channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: VCT EMEA Stage 1 Playoffs Preview: Who Will Make Valorant Masters London?
item.content:encoded:
VCT EMEA’s Stage 1 playoffs start on May 7, as the race for the Valorant Masters London spots begins in the EMEA region.

Three teams will make it to the second international of the year, and four teams will rack up those vital Champions Tour Points as they hope to make it to Valorant Champions 2026 in Shanghai.

Team Heretics eye pathway to Masters London

As VCT EMEA Stage 1 playoffs loom, the playoff picture is somewhat muddied by underperforming rosters and unexpected group stage triumphs.

FUT surprisingly starts in the Upper Semi-Finals after topping Group Alpha, meeting the winner of Team Heretics and Eternal Fire there.

ULF finished last at VCT EMEA Kickoff, but following the removal of the org from VCT and the transfer of four of those players to Eternal Fire, they are now firing on all cylinders, racking up an impressive 4-1 Group Stage record.

Still, the more seasoned Team Heretics roster will be looking at Eternal Fire and FUT as the dream pathway to Valorant Champions London, despite a middling 3-2 Group Stage showing in a bracket that feels somewhat mixed around from where commentators and analysts would have expected.

The event will be of special emphasis for British player Benjy ‘benjyfishy’ Fish, as he aims to play in front of his home crowd.

Are Fnatic ready for Playoff contention?

To make matters more confusing, the presumptive strongest team in the region, Fnatic, recently added Clément ‘CyvOph’ Millard following Sylvain ‘Veqaj’ Pattyn’s unexpected benching.

The result is that CyvOph enters the playoffs after only one showing in Fnatic orange, a 2-0 victory over BBL. While the Frenchman showed promising individual signs, it is unclear whether the team has had time to discuss additional layers like protocols, positioning, utility usage, and such.

Therefore, Fnatic’s dominance is less certain, though still expected. However, they will have to get through the winner of Vitality and Liquid.

Vitality barely scraped through to the playoffs following a 2-3 group stage record, but their individuals can never be underestimated, even with Fnatic’s strong record against Vitality.

As for Liquid, their 4-1 record in Group Alpha left them with the best map winrate in the group and the biggest round difference.

Liquid defeated Fnatic 3-0 to claim the final VCT MAsters Santiago spot, making the match another quest for vengeance for the Fnatic roster, but also a potentially tough matchup against Ayaz ‘nAt’ Akhmetshin’s men.

BBL and Gentle Mates start in Lower Bracket

Rounding out the playoffs are the top two teams of VCT EMEA Kickoff, BBL and Gentle Mates, both of whom will only get one life in the playoffs as they start from the Lower Bracket.

BBL were a revelation at VCT EMEA Kickoff, with their aggressive style taking several teams by surprise, but one wonders whether they have somewhat been figured out as the playoffs approach.

As the VCT EMEA Kickoff top two, the pair naturally have the most VCT Points in the EMEA region before the playoffs start, with BBL sitting on 5 and Gentle Mates on 3.

In addition to the top 3 making Valorant Masters London, the VCT EMEA Stage 1 playoffs will distribute the following VCT Points:

  • First Place: 6 points
  • Second Place: 4 points
  • Third place: 3 points
  • Fourth Place: 2 points

Who do you think will win VCT EMEA Stage 1? Discuss that on our Discord!
For more Valorant news, see the ticket info for the VCT EMEA Stage 2 Barcelona Finals.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Forza Horizon 6 Has Gone Gold Two Weeks Ahead of Release
item.content:encoded:
Playground Games has announced that Forza Horizon 6 has gone gold, leaving ample time for launch.

Forza Horizon 6 developers have been periodically revealing new details about the upcoming title as we wait for its launch. Just last week, we saw them inform players about the upcoming title’s multiplayer modes. Now, the team has announced an update on the title’s final development progress, as well as radio and accessibility details.

Forza Horizon 6 Hits Gold Status

Playground Games announced Forza Horizon 6’s Gold Status through a new post on X (formerly Twitter). For those unaware, when a game goes gold, it essentially means that the final product is ready for production. The developers announced this update along with new information on the game’s radio stations, accessibility features, and more.

Forza Horizon 6 Radio and Accessibility Features

Starting with Radio, the developers have announced that FH6 will feature the largest number of tracks yet in the series, with 9 radio stations, electronic anthems, energizing drum & bass, feel-good electropop, heavy-hitting rock, alternative indie, hip-hop beats, nostalgic synthwave, and modern classical music featured. Check out all the stations below:

  • Horizon Pulse
  • Horizon Bass Arena
  • Horizon Block Party
  • Horizon XS
  • Hospital Records
  • Gacha City Radio
  • Sub Pop Records
  • Horizon Wave
  • Horizon Opus

Regarding Forza Horizon 6’s accessibility, the game will include features such as AutoDrive, Proximity Radar to help identify cars in blind spots, and a high-contrast mode that helps distinguish objects more easily. Players will also see returning features such as full controller remapping, screen-reading narration, subtitles, adjustable camera field-of-view options on PC, color-blind filters, Offline Game Speed modification, and our full set of difficulty settings and driving assists.

Post-launch, players will see the addition of American Sign Language (ASL) and British Sign Language (BSL) for cinematics, with a picture-in-picture display of an ASL or BSL narrator.

In other news, a new report states that Forza Horizon 6’s pre-launch sales on Steam have already surpassed 500,000 copies. Additionally, here are all the details on game editions and pre-order bonuses. What are your thoughts on Forza Horizon 6 going gold? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the Fallout Nuka-Girl statue’s release window and price details. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Fallout Nuka Cola Girl Statue Release Window & Price Announced
item.content:encoded:
The fifth Fallout statue, Fallout: Nuka Girl, from GamingHeads is set to release between July and September 2026.

Fallout is easily one of the biggest gaming franchises out there, with several of the games in the series retaining players years after release. Bethesda recently spoke about why players keep coming back to Fallout 76 after all these years. If you are also a true enthusiast of the franchise, then the upcoming information will definitely excite you.

Fallout Nuka Cola Girl Statue Features, Release and Price Details Revealed

There are three different Fallout Nuka-Girl Statues on the way from GamingHeads: the exclusive edition, the regular edition, and the collective edition. The features of the exclusive edition and the regular edition are almost the same, with both being 13″ tall (⅙ scale) with a Nuka Cola bottle cap base.

Both statues depict Nuka-Girl wearing her signature Rocketsuit, with white slacks and a white crop top (both with red stripes running down them), black leather boots and gloves, a red jetpack, and a space helmet. The only difference is that the exclusive edition features a radiant sphere background. The exclusive edition costs $549.99 and is limited to only 500 units. At the same time, the regular edition costs $499.99 and is limited to 1,000 units.

The collective edition is a little different, still a 13″ tall (⅙ scale) statue, featuring the same apparel and base; however, it is unpainted. This edition costs $399.99 and is limited to 50 units only. All the statues feature a deluxe full-color box, individually hand-numbered base, and a validation card. Nuka-Girl can be showcased and displayed with or without the visor on her space helmet in all editions.

In other news, Bethesda has discussed the future of Fallout 76 and how the roadmap continues to evolve. Additionally, Fallout: New Vegas writer Chris Avellone has mentioned the term ‘New Vegas 2’, prompting short-lived hope in many Fallout fans. What are your thoughts on the Fallout Nuka-Cola statue? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Ubisoft’s Mark Rubin discussing the possibility of XDefiant returning. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
